experiment_A_helper_known_impostor.note: Fair 1-vs-1: both strategies pick 1 probe from same pool of candidates.

experiment_B_perm_linkability.interpretation: Mated Jaccard ≈ random (CTM randomization effective). Non-mated Jaccard < random (users have anti-correlated reliability patterns). The resulting gap enables EER < 50%: a genuine perm-based linkability concern. Mitigation: apply user-specific random masking to stored perm.

security_conclusions.A: perm gives slight advantage: check result
security_conclusions.B: Mated Jaccard ≈ random (+0.0028): CTM randomizes perm. Non-mated < random (-0.1251): users have anti-correlated reliability patterns. Perm EER=9.3% (not 50%: genuine finding, mitigatable via perm masking).
